query: Ukraine drone campaign Russian oil refineries strike rate June July 2026 - how many refinery strikes per month, is the campaign continuing to intensify, Moscow region drone attacks frequency
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's a summary of the key findings on Ukraine's drone campaign against Russian oil refineries for June–July 2026:

**Strike rate:** ~16 successful refinery strikes in May 2026 (26 across April–May combined per Carnegie). June 2026 maintained a comparable tempo of roughly one refinery strike every ~2 days. Cumulatively, 8 of Russia's 10 largest and 24 of ~33 major refineries have been hit.

**Is it intensifying?** Yes in reach and effect, less so in raw count:
- **Reach** grew from ~630 km (2022) to ~1,750–2,000 km (2026); drones now hit Tyumen and probe Omsk (2,500 km).
- **Effect**: refining fell below 4M bpd (21-year low); 20–33% of capacity offline; fuel rationing spread from ~17 regions (June 24) to 50+ (June 29); Putin acknowledged a fuel "deficit" for the first time.
- A late-May Meduza analysis found strikes "reaching twice as deep but not surging" in raw frequency — the key change is depth, precision (targeting distillation units), and Russia's declining repair ability.

**Moscow region:** The Kapotnya (Moscow) refinery — ~40% of the capital's fuel — was hit 2–3 times in June (June 6, 16, 18-19, including a ~200-drone assault causing "oil rain"). All four Moscow airports were closed by drone waves on June 16, June 18 (~527 flights disrupted), and June 22 (59 drones downed), with further disruptions June 30–July 1 — making Moscow-area attacks a near-weekly occurrence.

**Caveats:** Ukrainian claims (~40% offline) exceed independent estimates (~20–28%). Some strikes (Yaroslavl, Ufa) are unverified, and analysts judge the crisis unlikely to force near-term Russian concessions. Full details, data points, and sources have been recorded in the structured output.

sources.title: Ukrainian attack causes fire at southern Russia refinery (Slavyansk, Yaroslavl)
sources.snippet: Ukrainian drones struck two refineries June 28; Slavyansk fire killed at least two; 213 drones claimed intercepted.

sources.title: Russia's oil sector bruised but not broken by Ukraine's drone strikes
sources.snippet: 26 attacks on refining facilities in April–May; output fell up to 700,000 bpd (13%); Ryazan hit 15 times.

sources.title: Russia lost a third of refining due to Ukrainian NPZ attacks (Moscow Times)
sources.snippet: 16 successful refinery hits in May; refining below 4M bpd, 21-year low; ~1/3 capacity idle.

sources.title: How Ukraine's drone campaign sparked a fuel crisis 3,500km behind enemy lines
sources.snippet: Range up to ~1,750km; >20% refining capacity offline; crude output 8.7M bpd (-5% y/y).

sources.title: Ukrainian drones have struck nearly every major Russian refinery — which remain unhit?
sources.snippet: 8 of top-10 refineries struck by end-June 2026; only Omsk and Angarsk untouched.

sources.title: Meduza analysis: strikes reaching twice as deep but not surging in 2026
sources.snippet: Long-range strikes reach twice as deep but raw frequency not surging; refineries keep recovering.

sources.title: Russia Faces Growing Fuel Crunch as Ukrainian Strikes Knock Out Refineries
sources.snippet: 17 regions with mandatory restrictions June 24; Kapotnya offline until end-2026; >20% capacity offline; 8 of 10 largest hit.

sources.title: Fuel Shortages Spread Across 50+ Russian Regions as Refinery Strikes Knock Out Quarter of Output
sources.snippet: Shortages across 50+ regions as of June 29, 2026; ~quarter of output knocked out.

sources.title: Ukraine drone strike damages Moscow's largest oil refinery
sources.snippet: June 16 strike on Gazpromneft Moscow refinery; drone attacks on refineries doubled since start of 2026.

sources.title: Sobyanin: several drones reached Moscow refinery in Kapotnya
sources.snippet: Kapotnya hit multiple times (May 17, June 16); drone attacks on Moscow region increasing, forcing airport closures.

sources.title: Russia has 'black oil rain' after Ukraine's super drone attack
sources.snippet: June 18 ~200-drone attack, largest of war; Kapotnya hit 3rd time in a month; 4 Moscow airports closed.

sources.title: Wave of Ukrainian drones closes all Moscow airports
sources.snippet: 527 flights canceled/delayed; second Moscow refinery strike that week (June 18).

sources.title: Russia: four Moscow airports briefly closed due to drone waves
sources.snippet: June 22: 59 drones intercepted, all four Moscow airports closed 02:39–05:16 local.

sources.title: Russia's fourth-largest refinery on fire again (NORSI/Kstovo)
sources.snippet: July 2 strike on NORSI, second in two weeks; follows June 24 strike disabling CDU-5.

sources.title: Ukraine Drone Strikes Cripple Russian Refineries as Fuel Shortages Put Putin Under Pressure
sources.snippet: ~28% refining offline as of June 20 (Vakulenko/WSJ); Ufa struck second time in a week; fuel >100 rubles/liter.

sources.title: Only two of Russia's top-10 refineries remain undamaged
sources.snippet: Refining under 4M bpd early June, lowest in 2 decades; ~1/3 capacity offline; rationing in several regions.

sources.title: Eight of ten large refineries paralyzed by Ukraine
sources.snippet: May 2026: ≥30 successful attacks, 16 on refineries; June throughput ~3.95M bpd (-25% y/y); 56 regions rationing.

sources.title: Is Ukraine's campaign of targeting Russian refineries working?
sources.snippet: Fuel shortages serious but unlikely to force Putin into peace concessions.

sources.title: Russian Oil Sector Battered but Not Broken by Ukrainian Air Attacks
sources.snippet: Vakulenko analysis: refining resilience stretched thin; race between drones and repair teams.

sources.title: Kuibyshev refinery in Samara halted after Ukrainian drone attack
sources.snippet: June 10 strike shut two crude units; Samara hub refineries (Novokuibyshevsk, Syzran) also offline.
